name: breast OS link from the tumor growth-rate constant k_g (Weibull PH)
description: Parametric proportional-hazards link from a TGI metric to population overall survival, driven by the tumor GROWTH-RATE CONSTANT k_g (the post-nadir regrowth rate) rather than the early week-8 change. S(t) = exp(-(t/scale)^shape * exp(beta * k_g)); a faster-regrowing tumor raises the hazard. The non-default, tail-sensitive alternative to the breast week-8 link (structure.default=false), reached via the explicit survival_link= argument. A model with no regrowth (k_g undefined) maps to the baseline hazard. Population/trial-level only; values illustrative and unverified.

extraction.tier_rationale: Sets baseline median OS for a non-regrowing (k_g=0) breast tumor (longer than NSCLC).

parameters.label: log-hazard coefficient on the growth-rate constant k_g

extraction.tier_rationale: k_g is small (~0.015/wk), so its per-unit hazard coefficient is large; link strength varies across trials.

predictive_performance.population: independent breast cohort (illustrative; k_g typically out-discriminates early shrinkage)
